All students are welcome for the opportunity to learn more about diabetes or...

name: UW Relay for Life
description: Relay for Life is a committee where we come together to make a meaningful impact in the fight against cancer. Our mission is to raise funds, awareness, and hope in the battle against this devastating disease.

name: UWaterloo Meditates
description: UWaterloo Meditates is a meditation and breathwork club that brings stress management tools and techniques for personal and professional success through the scientific use of evidence-based breathing and meditation techniques, personal awareness seminars and community service projects. It's one of the coolest places to connect with yourself, make awesome friends, and, most importantly, have fun and be a part of an inclusive community!

name: Revolutionary Communist Party Club
description: The principal purpose of the Revolutionary Communist Party club is to gather University of Waterloo students, staff, faculty and members of the UW community at large and serve as a base for socialist politics on the UW campus.The activity and initiative of the Revolutionary Communist Party Club will be to give a socialist, and working class perspective and education on current (i.e. Gaza, Ukraine, US …) and historical events (i.e. Russian Revolution of 1917) to its members and to those who...

name: University of Waterloo Conservatives
description: University of Waterloo Conservatives is a group that connects university students to Conservatives parties. Students have the opportunity to volunteer on political campaigns, meet prominent Conservative politicians, and socialize with other Conservative youth. Past events have included hosting ministers, candidates, and having group socials.

name: Young Liberals, UW
description: The Young Liberals of Canada is the organization for progressive, politically-minded youth. The University of Waterloo Young Liberals, exists to help students, faculty and citizens get informed and engaged in Canadian politics. Our mission is to inform and inspire the next generation of political leaders in the Kitchener-Waterloo region and across Canada, and connect Canadian youth with the political process.

name: Campus Association for Baháʼí Studies at UW (CABS)
description: Campus Association for Baha'i Studies (CABS) is the UWaterloo club associated with the Baha'i Faith, a religion founded by Baha'u'llah whose central purpose is to establish the oneness of humanity.

name: Chinese Christian Fellowship, UW
description: The Chinese Christian Fellowship (CCF) is a campus group of culturally diverse Christians who meet together regularly to: be a visible and effective witness for Jesus Christ on this campus, provide an environment of mutual support that will enable students to discover their potential and the true meaning of life within the context of university as well as after graduation, encourage its members to practice genuine Christian love, and help students integrate into local churches.

name: Christian Orthodox Campus-Ministry Association
description: The Christian Orthodox Campus-Ministry Association (COCA) is our vibrant community dedicated to connecting university students and anyone curious about the Orthodox Christian faith to Christ and His Church. At COCA, we live out our mission by growing together in community, deepening our spiritual lives through prayer, serving others with love, and learning more about the Orthodox faith.

name: Christians on Campus
description: Christians on Campus (CoC) are believers in Christ. Thus, we love the Lord Jesus Christ and endeavor to give Him "the first place in all things" (Col. 1:18). Whether meeting one-on-one or in small groups, our desire is to cooperate with Him for the accomplishment of God's eternal purpose (Eph. 3:11) by ministering the unsearchable riches of Christ (Eph. 3:8) for the building up of the Body of Christ (Eph. 4:12). We warmly receive all believers in Christ, and people who want to learn about...

name: Eastern Orthodox Students Association (EOSA)
description: EOSA is a club for the Eastern Orthodox Christian students of UW to find fellowship and grow spiritually on campus. We will meet for prayer, homilies, social gatherings and more, in the hope that we can (a) draw closer together in Christ, and (b) sanctify not just our academic activities but the rest of our lives as well.

name: Hillel Waterloo
description: Hillel Waterloo strives to inspire every Jewish student in Waterloo to make an enduring commitment to Jewish life, learning, and Israel. Through promoting Jewish identity, cultivating student leadership and embracing religious and political diversity in an inclusive environment, Hillel Waterloo will enrich the lives of students on campus.

name: Hindu Students Council at the University of Waterloo
description: The Hindu Students' Council (HSC) is an international forum that provides opportunities to learn about Hindu heritage through various activities, events and projects. HSC presents ample opportunities for self-development at the spiritual and professional level.

name: Kingdom Come
description: We are a campus movement dedicated to helping students discover Jesus and mobilizing the next generation for the kingdom of God.Our primary focus is to help university and college students across Canada experience God’s love and forgiveness and to inspire them to influence others to become lifelong followers of Jesus.

name: Korean Christian Fellowship
description: We are a community of students passionate about knowing God and participating in his work on campus and in the world. Together we pursue transformation through Scripture to lead and equip others to join in Jesus' extraordinary revolution.

name: Power to Change (Campus Crusade for Christ)
description: Power to Change is a Christian club who exists to serve the spiritual needs of students on campus.   Anyone and everyone is welcome to our club--whether you are a Christian or not.   We would love to hear your life story.

name: Sikh Students Association (SSA)
description: The University of Waterloo Sikh Students Association (SSA) attempts to provide an environment on campus, and beyond, that helps to promote the Sikh religion by practicing its beliefs and traditions. The above is achieved through various discussion groups, weekly path sessions, kirtans, guest speakers, Q/A periods and a variety of religious/language classes provided by fellow students. As well, UW SSA attempts to participate in a wide range of multi-cultural activities both on campus, and...

name: TMA (Thaqalayn Muslim Association)
description: The Thaqalayn Muslim Association was founded in early 2012. The club was formed by a group Shia Ithna Asheri Muslim students in response to a lack of an official support network for the post-secondary Shia Muslim students in the Kitchener-Waterloo region. Although the headquarters of TMA Waterloo's activities is the University of Waterloo's main campus, the TMA aims to be accessible to all post-secondary students in the region. Thus, the TMA Waterloo’s purpose is to cater primarily, although...

name: University of Waterloo Soka Gakkai International (UWSGI)
description: The purpose of the club is to gather University of Waterloo students, staff, faculty, and members of the UWaterloo community at large to better understand Nichiren Daishonin’s Buddhism with the keen understanding that we live in a diverse environment. The Soka Gakkai is a diverse global community of individuals in 192 countries and territories who practice Nichiren Buddhism. It cherishes the value of peace, culture and education centered on the respect for the dignity of life. We meet weekly...

name: Youth Alive Fellowship
description: Youth Alive Fellowship is a club primarily designed to serve Christians on campus and to create a safe space for service to God. The purpose of the club is to make the Youth effective witnesses for Christ.

name: ACI Student Design Team
description: Students compete against teams from around the world at the American Concrete Institute (ACI) Spring and Fall Conventions.

name: Baja SAE
description: The University of Waterloo Baja SAE team designs and builds an off-road, single seat vehicle to compete annually at SAE sanctioned Baja competitions.

name: Battery Workforce Challenge
description: We are a student-run design team focused on designing, building, and integrating an electric vehicle (EV) battery pack into a Ram ProMaster cargo van.

name: Electrium Mobility
description: Electrium Mobility is a student design team at the University of Waterloo dedicated to building sustainable, portable, and electric transportation solutions in the form of electric skateboards, bikes, scooters, and other portable electric mobility options.

name: Engineers Without Borders (EWB)
description: Engineers Without Borders (EWB) is an non-governmental organization founded by Waterloo graduates that work towards ending poverty within the world.

name: Formula Nano
description: Formula Nano is dedicated to designing, building, and racing molecular machines at the nanoscale.

name: F_RMlab
description: F_RMlab is a design and research collective engaged in advanced computational design tools in support of new paradigms of public space where dynamic interaction, social responsiveness, regenerative materials and systemic resilience are integral goals in building.

name: Industry 4.0
description: The University of Waterloo Industry 4.0 design team is focused on promoting the unique Management Engineering program offered here at the University.

name: International Genetically Engineered Machine team (iGEM)
description: The goal of the Waterloo International Genetically Engineered Machine team (iGEM) is to engineer biological processes like electrical and software systems, rewiring naturally-occurring genetic components using principles from synthetic biology.

name: MedTechResolve
description: MedTechResolve aims to automate the process of diagnosis, treatment, and prognosis, both inside and outside of the emergency room (ER).

name: Midnight Sun Solar Car Team
description: The Midnight Sun Solar Rayce Car Team designs, manufactures, and races solar-electric cars capable of travelling on public highways.

name: Robotics Team (UWRT)
description: The uWaterloo Robotics Team focuses on the constantly evolving field of autonomous vehicles and the team frequently takes on multiple projects and competitions each year.

name: Steel Bridge Design Team
description: The Steel Bridge Design Team's focus is on designing, fabricating, and competing with a modular steel bridge at an annual international competition.

name: UW Biomechatronics Design Team (Biotron)
description: The UW Biomechatronics Design Team (aka Biotron) is a design team at the University of Waterloo that designs and builds biomechanical devices to solve biomedical problems.

name: UW Blueprint
description: UW Blueprint strives to make technology accessible and useful for those who create communities and promote public welfare.

name: UW Concrete Canoe
description: The UW Concrete Canoe Team designs, builds, and races a canoe made entirely of reinforced concrete for the annual Canadian National Concrete Canoe Competition (CNCCC).

name: UW Concrete Toboggan
description: The Waterloo Toboggan Team (est. 1984) designs and builds a 5-person sled with steering, braking, suspension, and concrete skis, then races it against other universities in Canada's oldest and largest student engineering competition; the Great Northern Concrete Toboggan Race (GNCTR).

name: UW Formula Electric (UWFE)
description: University of Waterloo Formula Electric (UWFE, formerly known as Waterloo Formula Electric and UW Formula Motorsports), is a student design team dedicated to designing, building, testing, and competing with a formula-style open-wheel electric racecar each year in the FSAE Michigan and Formula Hybrid + Electric competition series.

name: UW Nanorobotics Group
description: UWNRG is a student-run design team aiming to solve modern problems with nano solutions.

name: UW Orbital
description: UW Orbital is a group of determined individuals, of all different skill levels and programs, coming together to build a CubeSat with the goal of competing in the upcoming Canadian Satellite Design Challenge (CSDC).

name: UW REACT
description: UW REACT keeps the spirit of theFIRST Robotics Competitionalive after high school by building fully autonomous FRC robots.

name: UW Reality Labs
description: UW Reality Labs is a student design team at the University of Waterloo that specializes in XR (extended reality) and spatial computing.

name: UW VEX U Robotics
description: The UW VEX U Robotics design team; designs, builds and programs robots to compete in the international VEX U Robotics competition.

name: UWAFT (University of Waterloo Alternative Fuel Team)
description: At UWAFT (University of Waterloo Alternative Fuel Team) we continuously break new ground in the field of sustainable transportation through innovation, technology, and student engagement.

name: UWASIC
description: UWASIC is a student design team at the University of Waterloo dedicated to exploring the world of ASIC and FPGA development.

name: UWaterloo IISE
description: IISE (Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers) is the world's largest professional society dedicated to the support of the industrial & systems engineering profession and individuals involved with improving quality and productivity. ​

name: UWSiO
description: UWSiO (University of Waterloo Silicon Integrated Optics) is a dynamic group of students focused on advancing and revolutionizing data storage through the use of silica systems.

name: Warrior Home Design Team
description: The Warrior Home Design Team is a multidisciplinary student design team at the University of Waterloo working towards implementing a high-performance, sustainable, and net-zero energy building in the affordable housing market.

name: Wat Street
description: Wat Street is a student-run Design Team at the University of Waterloo that strives to provide students with a comprehensive education in the fields of financial investment strategies and algorithm development.

name: WAT.ai
description: WAT.ai's goal is to establish an environment to enable the continued growth of AI talent and suitable access to opportunities within the waterloo community.

name: WatArrow
description: WatArrow is a new design team at the University of Waterloo that focuses on the design of small airplanes to compete in the SAE Aero Design competition.

name: WATBOTs
description: WATBOTs is Waterloo’s mini BattleBot team, dedicated to designing, building, and competing with 1–3 pound combat robots.

name: Waterloo Aerial Robotics Group (WARG)
description: The Waterloo Aerial Robotics Group (WARG) is a student team focused on innovating the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle industry.

name: Waterloo Alternative Protein Project
description: The Waterloo Alternative Protein Project is the first chapter of the Good Food Institute’s Alt. Protein Project in Canada. The project aims to create a sustainable and secure food system through research, entrepreneurship, and innovation.

name: Waterloo Hacker Fab
description: Waterloo Hacker Fab is establishing Canada’s first open-source microchip fabrication lab, with all equipment engineered from scratch.

name: Waterloo Rocketry
description: Waterloo Rocketry builds liquid sounding rockets that soar to 38,000 feet, reaching speeds as fast as Mach 2.2 and breaking records! The team designs and builds their own rocket engines, airframes, controls systems, and more.

name: Waterloo Space Research Team (WSRT)
description: UW Space Research empowers undergraduate students to explore the frontiers of space research through hands-on design, engineering, and experimentation.

name: Waterloop
description: The Waterloop project is the response by a group of dedicated University of Waterloo students to the SpaceX Hyperloop pod challenge to independently design and build a prototype Hyperloop pod to be tested on a one-mile test track in Hawthorne, California in the summer of 2016.

name: Waterloo RoboSoccer Team (UWRS)
description: The Waterloo RoboSoccer Team (UWRS) develops autonomous humanoid robots to compete in 4-on-4 soccer matches at the annual RoboCup competition.

name: WATFlow
description: WATFlow is a student design team at the University of Waterloo focused on developing innovative water and wastewater solutions that support sustainable urban water management.

name: WATOLINK
description: WATOLINK offers students an opportunity to develop applications involving action-classification via EEG signal analysis and inference.

name: WATonomous
description: WATonomous is the flagship autonomy design team at the University of Waterloo.
